titleStmt.title: Letter from Cis M. Coyle to James Ryan, 2 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Cis M. Coyle

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Cis M Coyle to James Ryan (1891-1970) on 2 July 1916. was written after Ryan was transferred from Stafford to Frongach in Wales. The writer refers to other prisoners with whom whom she is friendly and asks about the camp rules in Frongach.James Ryan studied medicine in UCD. He was appointed chief medical officer in the GPO during the Easter Rising. After the Rising he was imprisoned in Stafford and, later, Frongoch and was released in August 1916. Ryan was later a founder member of Fianna Fáil and a government minister.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
58 Aughrim St July 2nd. 1916 My dear Jim Your letter of June 26th
. received yesterday morning
for which many thanks.
You are I presume in
Frongoch by this time
and I hope you find
the change pleasant I know you can write
but one letter per week
now and of course you
will send that one home
naturally, but I will
write to you all the same
and when Michael is
writing home if he says
how you are etc. his sister
will let me know. She &
I are great friends. I am
expecting a letter from Liam
I wonder he has not
written. I don't know what
are the rules of the camps,
& are you allowed to
smoke? I wonder do you
know a friend of mine
Peter Moran only a day
or two ago I found out
that he had been in
Stafford. I thought he was
in Wandsworth and asked
Liam to look him up. He
is now in Frongoch. The
Councillor is laid up
with influenza in Wandsworth
but I suppose he will
join you later on. It
will be quite a
meeting for you all. I
am glad your sister has
been to see you, a visit
from home must be a
great treat to you at
present. I want you to
look up S. Donnelly
Wexford St. I don't know him
but his brother is a friend
of mine and some of them
may be going over to Wales
later on. If so I will get
them to see my friends
also. I am told that you
have the use of a piano
in Camp & that you have
concerts amongst yourselves
Is it true I wonder. If
it is ask Peter Moran to
sing a few ?comic? songs
for you. Tell Liam to
write to me & tell Michael
I was enquiring for him.
I will write again when
I hear some news from the
Camp. Kindest regards
from all. Very Sincerely Yours Cis M.Coyle
